message:
ftui-harness: add 92 tests for trace_replay, determinism, time_travel_inspector

Addresses coverage gaps identified in the bd-1av4o testing epic:

- trace_replay.rs: 54 unit tests (FNV-1a hash, TraceGrid, parse_* helpers,
  read_cell, apply_diff_runs, apply_full_buffer) + 10 new integration tests
  (full_buffer_v1, resize between frames, error cases, grapheme cells)
- determinism.rs: 29 new tests (escape_json, json_string, env helpers,
  EnvSnapshot builder, JsonValue variants, logger seq/schema/context/override,
  emit_line JSON validity)
- time_travel_inspector.rs: 9 new tests (empty TimeTravel, header formatting
  with/without model_hash, navigation, frame dimensions)

Total ftui-harness lib tests: 256 (was 164).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ai1.4","title":"Write ADR for SDK modularization","description":"Record the rationale, options, and decisions around embedded core vs host bindings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Write ADR for SDK modularization
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:15:57.982900618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:15:59.847374803Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:15:59.847348173Z","close_reason":"ADR written in docs/adr/ADR-007-sdk-modularization.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","docs","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1ai1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1ai1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1ai1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1ai1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1ai1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":302,"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ADR Contents
- Context: why not use C as universal bridge.
- Decision: separate host-specific FFI crates.
- Consequences: more crates but lower coupling.
- Alternatives: monolithic crate, C ABI core, or dynamic plugin model.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:16:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ai1.5","title":"Define test strategy for embedded core + host bindings","description":"Specify unit and integration test strategy for future embedded core and each host-specific FFI crate (no implementation yet).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Define test strategy for embedded core + host bindings
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)
","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:20:18.148412777Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:13:08.891008366Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:13:08.886348598Z","close_reason":"Test strategy drafted in docs/spec/sdk-test-strategy.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","sdk","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ai1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ai1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ai1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":303,"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Define how to test an embedded core crate (pure logic + IO traits).
- Define per-host integration test expectations (WASM, C, Zig, JVM).
- Identify logging/diagnostics needs for FFI issues.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clear mapping of test types to crates and build targets.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:20:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1are","title":"Update README to reflect implemented crates and current status","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-02-02T02:21:30.222938740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:22:32.409001310Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:22:32.408974479Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1av4o","title":"Testing: Full Coverage (No Mocks) + E2E Determinism/Logging","description":"Goal: Provide comprehensive unit coverage WITHOUT mocks/fakes and complete E2E integration scripts with deterministic fixtures + rich JSONL logging.

Approach:
- Use real harnesses (ProgramSimulator, PTY, render buffers) and deterministic seeds/time steps.
- Centralize fixtures (seed/run_id/tick/env capture) for tests + scripts.
- Define and validate a JSONL log schema; ensure every screen has deterministic E2E coverage.
- Expand coverage across core/runtime/render/text/layout/style/widgets/extras/harness/pty.
- Update coverage docs and keep CI aligned with coverage gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Pre-change and post-change coverage runs complete (or blockers documented) with docs updated.
2) program.rs coverage is materially increased (target >= 75%) using ProgramSimulator tests (no mocks).
3) Coverage gaps closed for core/runtime/render/text/layout/style/widgets/extras/harness/pty with deterministic assertions.
4) Identified coverage gaps (image.rs, pty_capture, terminal_session, log_viewer, input, virtualized, block, text.rs, frame.rs, terminal_model, diff.rs, presenter.rs) have real tests.
5) E2E fixtures standardize seeds/run_id/tick/env; VFX harness seed configurable + logged.
6) E2E JSONL schema defined + validation step added; all E2E scripts emit consistent, detailed logs.
7) E2E screen sweep coverage matrix completed with missing scenarios implemented.
8) No mocks/fakes introduced; any unavoidable simulation is documented and justified.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"FrostyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:37:02.752299871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:26:00.622652144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":631,"issue_id":"bd-1av4o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Audit snapshot:
- rg shows stubs/fakes in widgets (panel_stub), demo caps_probe_stub, and fake IDs in render pools; not necessarily test mocks but indicates coverage gaps vs "no mocks" goal.
- E2E logging exists (tests/e2e/lib/logging.sh + scripts), but schema validation and screen sweep coverage are incomplete.

Plan (no-mocks path):
1) Capture baseline coverage + blockers.
2) Expand real-harness tests (ProgramSimulator + PTY + render buffers).
3) Add deterministic fixture utilities (seed/time/env/run_id) for tests + scripts.
4) Define JSONL schema + validation gate; ensure logs include run_id/seed/env/tty caps/durations.
5) Build E2E screen sweep matrix (alt/inline × sizes × key screens).
6) Audit/remove mock/fake usage (replace with real harnesses or document exception).

I’ll keep dependencies aligned so final coverage run only after all test+logging tasks complete.","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:05:26Z"}]}

- Build a very large, dense sample that stresses routing, label placement, and layout convergence.

- Metadata marks it as PERF/STRESS and includes expected layout time budget.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:25.063096029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:45:12.679457554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:45:12.679438529Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mermaid","samples"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.5.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:25.063096029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":780,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.5.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BrightSnow claiming. Batching all sample set beads together.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:42:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig.6","title":"State model + action handling for mega screen","description":"Goal:
- Implement the state machine that backs all mega screen controls.

Deliverables:
- State struct covering sample selection, complexity controls, layout knobs, viewport sizing, overlay toggles, auto-run mode, and last-run metrics.
- Action enum + handler with deterministic updates, clamp rules, and logging.
- Reset-to-defaults action with full state snapshot.

Acceptance:
- Every keybinding updates state predictably and is reflected in UI panels.
- State changes are recorded in the status log for debugging.
- Unit test exercises boundary clamps and reset behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:35.537506408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:48:13.444457889Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:09:30.451022928Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mermaid","showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:35.537506408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:27.868603009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:32.460608301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":755,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed as part of bd-3oaig.3 mega screen work + status log addition. MermaidMegaState has 18 fields (17 control fields + status_log). MegaAction enum has 26 variants. apply() handler logs every action via StatusLogEntry with 64-entry cap. All keybindings update state deterministically and are reflected in UI panels. 16 tests passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:09:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig.7","title":"Rendering pipeline integration + cache invalidation","description":"Goal:
- Wire mega screen rendering to Mermaid parse/layout/render pipeline with explicit cache invalidation.

Deliverables:
- Cache keyed by (sample id, size, layout mode, render options, seed).
- Deterministic invalidation when controls change.
- Cache stats (hits/misses, evictions) exposed to UI and logs.

Acceptance:
- Re-render only occurs when inputs change; cached frames are reused otherwise.
- Cache state is visible in the metrics panel and included in structured logs.
- Unit test verifies invalidation for each control group.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:39.985281747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:48:19.362139953Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:18:26.910324906Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mermaid","render","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:39.985281747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:36.659120972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:39.102918339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":758,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented render pipeline integration and cache invalidation for the Mermaid Mega Showcase screen:

- Added RefCell<MegaRenderCache> to MermaidMegaShowcaseScreen for interior mutability (view takes &self)
- Implemented ensure_render_cache() with 3-tier invalidation: analysis (parse+IR), layout, render — matching the existing mermaid_showcase.rs pattern
- Implemented blit_buffer() for centering the cached buffer onto the frame
- Added 10 curated inline Mermaid samples (Flow Basic/Subgraphs/Dense, Sequence, Class, State, ER, Pie, Gantt, Mindmap)
- Added to_config() layout mode support (Dense=400 iter budget, Spacious=140)
- Added layout_spacing() with Dense (tight 2.0 gaps) and Spacious (loose 6.0 gaps)
- Updated side panel to display cache metrics: parse/layout/render timing, cache hits/misses, last cache status
- Replaced render_diagram_placeholder with render_diagram using the full pipeline
- Added 7 new tests (mega_samples_non_empty, selected_source_wraps_around, render_cache_starts_empty, ensure_cache_populates_on_first_call, ensure_cache_hits_on_repeat, config_dense_has_higher_budget, layout_spacing_dense_tighter)
- All 23 mega showcase tests + all 1155 demo showcase tests pass, clippy clean","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:18:22Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3oaig.9","title":"Optimal layout mode + auto-fit/auto-scale","description":"Goal:
- Provide an "optimal layout" mode that chooses spacing and scale to best fit the viewport.

Deliverables:
- Auto-fit heuristics (minimize overflow, reduce edge crossings if available).
- Layout quality scoring (overflow, crossings, edge length variance) used to pick the best candidate.
- Toggle for auto-fit vs manual size.

Acceptance:
- Switching to optimal mode recomputes layout and visibly improves fit in most cases.
- Quality scores are surfaced in the metrics panel for transparency.
- Auto-fit behavior is deterministic and documented in the help panel.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:48.557849994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:38.337127619Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","mermaid","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:48.557849994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:55.661961616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:52.827013258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3obms","title":"Improved Crossing Minimization (Sifting Algorithm)","description":"## Goal
Augment or replace the barycenter heuristic in diagram_layout.rs with the sifting algorithm for better edge crossing reduction. The existing barycenter approach is correct but produces suboptimal layouts for dense graphs (10+ nodes per layer).

## Background
- Current: barycenter_sort does bidirectional sweeps using neighbor position averages. O(n*E) per sweep, good results for sparse graphs.
- Sifting: for each node in a layer, try inserting it at every position and pick the one with minimum crossings. O(n^2 * E) per layer but finds significantly better solutions.
- The layout quality metrics already measure crossings — improvement is directly measurable.

## Implementation
1. Add sifting_sort() alongside barycenter_sort() in diagram_layout.rs.
2. For each layer, for each node: remove from position, count crossings at each alternate position, insert at best position.
3. Use the optimized O(1) crossing counter (the ref_member boolean array) from the barycenter fix.
4. Run sifting after barycenter (as a refinement pass) rather than as a replacement.
5. Budget-gated: sifting is more expensive; respect the computation budget.

## Expected Improvement
- 15-30% fewer crossings on graphs with 20+ nodes (based on graph drawing literature).
- No change for simple graphs (barycenter already optimal for small inputs).

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs (add sifting_sort, integrate with minimize_crossings)

## Tests
- Unit: sifting produces <= crossings compared to barycenter alone
- Property: sifting output is a valid permutation of the layer
- Benchmark: measure crossing reduction on 10 test graphs","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:29:48.481508990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:37:04.535774898Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:37:04.535753317Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","mermaid","optimization"],"comments":[{"id":791,"issue_id":"bd-3obms","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will implement the sifting algorithm as a refinement pass after barycenter sort in the crossing minimization pipeline.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:30:11Z"},{"id":792,"issue_id":"bd-3obms","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear: Implementation complete. Added:
1. count_crossings_pair() - counts crossings between a single adjacent layer pair
2. count_crossings_at() - counts crossings involving a specific layer (both adjacent pairs)
3. sifting_sort() - for each node in a layer, tries reinserting at every position and picks the one with minimum crossings. Budget-gated, only runs on layers with 3+ nodes
4. Integration in minimize_crossings() - sifting runs as a refinement pass after each barycenter sweep iteration, only when budget allows and crossings remain
5. 7 new tests: valid permutation, crossing reduction, budget respect, small layer noop, pipeline integration, dense graph improvement, pair counting consistency

All 505 ftui-extras tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:36:59Z"}]}
